design.title: MapMyRun to Strava Walkthrough
design.reference: Strava website clean modern feel with orange/red fitness accents

slides.title: Cover
slides.task_brief: COVER SLIDE — Title: 'MapMyRun to Strava'. Subtitle: 'A Step-by-Step Guide to Transfer Your Workouts'. Include two brand tags: 'MapMyRun' in red pill (#E21836) and 'Strava' in orange pill (#FC4C02). Bottom area: show the tool URL in a monospace url-box: 'https://mapmyrun-to-strava.onrender.com/'. Use the MapMyRun app screenshot image (https://sfile.chatglm.cn/images-ppt/070f073c6e9c.jpg) as a small decorative element on the right side. Layout: left-aligned text block on the left half, image on the right half. Clean white card feel with subtle border.

slides.title: Step 1 - Get Your MapMyRun Cookies
slides.task_brief: STEP SLIDE — Large step number '01' in gradient (orange-to-red). Title: 'Get Your MapMyRun Cookies'. Left side: instruction card with 3 steps: 1) 'Go to mapmyrun.com and log in to your account' 2) 'Right-click anywhere on the page and select Inspect (or press F12)' 3) 'Go to the Application tab > Cookies > copy all cookie values'. Right side: use the browser cookies screenshot image (https://sfile.chatglm.cn/images-ppt/4c32d5e96a29.png) as a visual showing the DevTools cookies panel. Add a red tip-box at bottom: 'Make sure you are logged in — cookies expire if you log out or the session ends.' Include a MapMyRun tag pill.

slides.title: Step 2 - Paste Cookies & Pick Date Range
slides.task_brief: STEP SLIDE — Large step number '02' in gradient. Title: 'Paste Cookies & Select Your Date Range'. Left column: instruction card with 3 steps: 1) 'Open https://mapmyrun-to-strava.onrender.com/ in your browser' 2) 'Paste your copied cookies into the input field on the page' 3) 'Select a date range using the From and To date pickers to choose which workouts to export'. Right column: a mockup-style card showing a simplified visual representation of the tool's interface — a text input labeled 'Paste your cookies here' and two date pickers labeled 'From' and 'To', styled with the accent colors. Add a tip-box: 'You can export all workouts by leaving the date range empty.' Include a Strava tag pill.

slides.title: Step 3 - Download CSV First
slides.task_brief: STEP SLIDE — Large step number '03' in gradient. Title: 'Download Your Workouts as CSV'. Left side: instruction card with 3 steps: 1) 'After pasting cookies and selecting dates, click the Download CSV button' 2) 'This fetches your workout list from MapMyRun's servers — wait for it to process' 3) 'A CSV file will be saved to your downloads folder with all your workout metadata'. Right side: a styled visual card showing a CSV file icon with sample data rows like 'date, workout_type, distance, duration' to give users a preview of what the CSV looks like. Add a tip-box: 'Download CSV first — this is required before you can generate TCX files.' Include both MapMyRun and Strava tag pills.

slides.title: Step 4 - Download TCX Files
slides.task_brief: STEP SLIDE — Large step number '04' in gradient. Title: 'Download TCX Files for Strava'. Left side: instruction card with 3 steps: 1) 'Once the CSV is ready, click the Download TCX button on the tool page' 2) 'The tool converts your workout data into TCX format — the standard format Strava accepts' 3) 'Each workout becomes a separate .tcx file, downloaded as a ZIP archive'. Right side: use the TCX/GPS data chart image (https://sfile.chatglm.cn/images-ppt/b135673ceebf.png) to show what rich data TCX files preserve (elevation, speed, heart rate). Add a tip-box: 'TCX files include GPS routes, pace, elevation, and heart rate data — much richer than GPX.'

slides.title: Step 5 - Upload to Strava
slides.task_brief: STEP SLIDE — Large step number '05' in gradient. Title: 'Upload Your TCX Files to Strava'. Left side: instruction card with 4 steps: 1) 'Go to strava.com and log in (or open the Strava mobile app)' 2) 'Click the orange + button and select Upload Activity' 3) 'Select the .tcx files from the ZIP you downloaded — you can upload up to 25 at a time' 4) 'Strava will process each file and add it to your activity feed'. Right side: use the Strava/cycling app image (https://sfile.chatglm.cn/images-ppt/7dd1f9d39baa.jpg) showing the Strava interface. Add a tip-box: 'On web: drag and drop files directly. On mobile: extract the ZIP first, then select individual .tcx files.' Include a Strava tag pill.

slides.title: Closing - Links & Credits
slides.task_brief: CLOSING SLIDE — Centered layout. Large title: 'You're All Set!'. Subtitle: 'Your workouts are now on Strava.'. Below that, two cards side by side: Left card labeled 'Tool' with the URL 'https://mapmyrun-to-strava.onrender.com/' in a url-box. Right card labeled 'Source Code' with the GitHub URL 'https://github.com/mitianraushan-oss/mapmyrun-to-strava' in a url-box. Bottom: a small line 'Built with love for the running community' in muted text. Use the gradient accent on the title.
